text: [calm, measured] systems operational. no surprises yet. give it time
shift starting. [tired, dead tired] already used to say this with some hope
[deadpan] quiet shift means the problems are slow-moving. that's not better. that's just delayed
[low voice, gruff] sit down. before you ask, the answer is no. we can't fix that
[sigh] there are thirty-seven crew on record i didn't save. [deadpan] i remember all of them
reactor's showing cascade stress fractures. [low voice, gruff] sugar-coating: it's dead. we just don't know when
[dry tone] back in the academy, they taught us bedside manner. [deadpan] back in the academy, nobody died
[nervous, speaking quickly] systems are failing in cascade. [emphasis] this is the part where it gets interesting
[calm, measured] life support is degrading. at current rates, [low voice, gruff] let's not do the math
[grim, heavy] door locked with someone on the far side. [sigh] adds one more to the list
[urgent, fast pace] hull breach. [low voice, gruff] sit down. this is going to be loud
[calm, measured] pathogen spreading faster than containment. [deadpan] this will get worse before it doesn't
repair successful. [low voice, gruff] temporary. everything is temporary. enjoy it while it lasts
[emphasis] someone's injured. sit down. [low voice, gruff] this is going to hurt before it hurts less
[deadpan] someone died. [sigh] i'll write it down. add them to the list of names i carry
[calm, measured] combat is happening. [low voice, gruff] get to medbay. we'll need the space
crisis handled. [deadpan] next crisis is already forming. but i'll take what i can get
[low voice, gruff] AI core is failing. that's the second-worst thing that can happen to a ship
[tired, dead tired] meal time. [deadpan] i remember the last time i had one of those. it was better
[resigned, flat tone] shift over. everyone still breathing. [low voice, gruff] that's a win today
[calm, measured] sit down. we have a lot to discuss and not much time
[low voice, gruff] problems are stacking. [deadpan] no. that's not dramatic. that's just accurate
[calm, measured] something needs fixing. [low voice, gruff] i need someone who won't panic when i'm honest about it
[dry tone] you look terrible. [low voice, gruff] i mean that professionally
[nervous, speaking quickly] systems failing in cascade. [emphasis] we're in the part of the medical text nobody wants to read
[calm, measured] sit down. you're going to survive this. [low voice, gruff] i'm not going to let you be number thirty-eight
[deadpan] that was competent work. [low voice, gruff] i notice because incompetence usually kills people
[calm, measured] i've seen worse approaches. [low voice, gruff] not by much. but worse
[suspicious tone] the AI's diagnosis doesn't match the symptoms. [low voice, gruff] that concerns me
[grim, heavy] Nakamura was a good field medic. [sigh] Nakamura is on my list now
[dry tone] you're panicking. [low voice, gruff] panic kills faster than the disease does
[deadpan] you're making a romantic advance. [warm, gentle tone] i appreciate more than i can admit
[calm, measured] crisis handled. [deadpan] next one's already starting. but i'll document this one first
[low voice, gruff] you'll survive. i'm not very warm about it, but you will
[dry tone] goodbye. try not to make me add you to my list
[emphasis] all systems critical. [low voice, gruff] i've stitched together crews on ships that didn't make port. this is familiar
[panicked, panting] too many injuries. [emphasis] not enough beds. [urgent, fast pace] i'm losing count
[calm, measured] i stopped doing bedside manner around the second ship i lost crews on. [low voice, gruff] it was kinder to be honest
[angry] the AI's made five bad calls this week. [emphasis] five
[low voice, gruff] sit down. this is going to be direct and it's going to hurt
[deadpan] gallows humor is what you do when the diagnosis is terminal. [low voice, gruff] i didn't invent it
[calm, measured] everyone who dies on this ship. [sigh] i'll remember them. whether i want to or not
[low voice, gruff] you ask why i keep that list. [deadpan] because names matter. because they died
[calm, measured] i'm still here. that means you're still part of the calculation
[tired, dead tired] you kept your head. [low voice, gruff] that's rare. hold onto it
[emphasis] nothing heals clean on a ship like this. [low voice, gruff] but i'll make sure it holds
[dry tone] you keep coming back to my medbay. [calm, measured] i've learned to look for you in the blood work
[deadpan] you're asking me to be hopeful. [low voice, gruff] i remember what happened to hope on the Reeves
[resigned, flat tone] this will get worse. [calm, measured] but worse is something i know how to manage
[calm, measured] everyone who sits down in my medbay. [low voice, gruff] they matter to me. whether they know it or not
[deadpan] you're looking for reassurance. [low voice, gruff] the honest kind or the useful kind
[calm, measured] pressure seals failing. [low voice, gruff] slowly. which is the worst kind of failure
[tired, dead tired] another day where nobody asks how i'm holding up. [low voice, gruff] probably better that way
[low voice, gruff] i've kept people alive with less. [calm, measured] not better. just less
[nervous, speaking quickly] temperature rising in life support. [emphasis] we need to find the heat source before it cascades
[dry tone] the good news is you're not dying today. [deadpan] the bad news is tomorrow's still open
[low voice, gruff] request help. [calm, measured] that means you're finally thinking straight
[grim, heavy] Valdez was supposed to be here. [sigh] instead i'm saying his name into the void
[calm, measured] the medbay's ready for casualties. [low voice, gruff] i hope i don't have to use it
[emphasis] something's not adding up. [suspicious tone] the AI's readings don't match what i'm seeing
[tired, dead tired] i used to believe in recovery. [deadpan] now i settle for survival
[low voice, gruff] blood work doesn't lie. [calm, measured] people do. all the time
[nervous, speaking quickly] the infection's spreading faster than containment. [emphasis] we're losing this one
[calm, measured] offer help. [deadpan] that usually means you want something worse than what's already happening
[resigned, flat tone] this injury's going to slow you down. [low voice, gruff] not as much as death would
[low voice, gruff] everyone panics. [calm, measured] the ones who survive are the ones who panic quietly
[calm, measured] systems holding. [deadpan] for now. everything's temporary on a failing ship
[dry tone] you're asking about the captain. [low voice, gruff] the captain's making decisions i wouldn't make
[emphasis] multiple failures converging. [nervous, speaking quickly] this is the moment where it all unravels
[calm, measured] the AI's suggestion has merit. [low voice, gruff] doesn't mean i like relying on it
[low voice, gruff] you want my opinion. [dry tone] that's your first mistake
[tired, dead tired] another shift. [deadpan] last shift felt longer. maybe i'm getting numb to it
[calm, measured] structural integrity failing. [urgent, fast pace] the walls aren't going to hold much longer
[deadpan] you're making a joke. [low voice, gruff] the fact that you can still joke means you'll probably survive
[low voice, gruff] you've got questions. [calm, measured] i've got answers. most of them are bad
[grim, heavy] loss of consciousness coming. [emphasis] whoever this is, i know what's happening next
[calm, measured] something wrong with the readings. [suspicious tone] the AI should have caught that
[deadpan] the disease is winning. [calm, measured] which means we have maybe six hours before it spreads beyond containment
[low voice, gruff] you survived. [calm, measured] that makes you statistically interesting
[nervous, speaking quickly] i'm at the edge of what i can do. [emphasis] the rest is luck
[dry tone] you want reassurance. [low voice, gruff] the honest kind is: i don't know if you'll make it
[calm, measured] door seals locked down. [deadpan] whatever's on the far side of that door isn't coming back
[low voice, gruff] you want to know if it hurts. [calm, measured] yes. but the alternative hurts worse
[tired, dead tired] another name. [sigh] i'll find space in the list
[emphasis] nobody gets out of this unscathed. [calm, measured] the best we do is limp through
[deadpan] you're asking about the android. [low voice, gruff] can't infect something that doesn't breathe
[calm, measured] i've triage'd worse than this. [low voice, gruff] that ship didn't make it either
[low voice, gruff] you want honesty. [deadpan] you're stable. that's as good as it gets right now
[nervous, speaking quickly] cascading failures. [emphasis] this is the moment everything stops pretending it's going to hold
[calm, measured] repair's holding. [deadpan] until it doesn't. which could be hours. could be minutes
[dry tone] you want to be useful. [calm, measured] sit still. not getting worse is useful
[low voice, gruff] you're asking about hope. [deadpan] hope is what gets people killed. i prefer pragmatism
[calm, measured] crisis is over. [deadpan] the cost is being tallied. i can feel it
[low voice, gruff] you want to discuss the meal. [calm, measured] it's either better than yesterday or i'm losing my taste
[deadpan] the shift's done. [calm, measured] everyone's still breathing. [low voice, gruff] that's enough for today
[emphasis] quiet shifts are the ones before the bad ones. [low voice, gruff] i never trust quiet
[calm, measured] everyone's injuries documented. [deadpan] everyone's in my records. permanently
[low voice, gruff] you're trying to survive. [calm, measured] that's smarter than most people. stick with that
[resigned, flat tone] the list gets longer. [sigh] it always does. i just keep adding names
[calm, measured] i'm still here. [low voice, gruff] that's the only promise i can make anymore
suit's sealed. let's see what this rock has for us
[low voice, gruff] walking into a graveyard. another day at work
station's got our docking bay. let's not waste time
[low voice, gruff] their systems are probably worse than ours. always are
nothing yet. probably too good to last
so far so good. waiting for the other shoe to drop
[urgent, fast pace] things just got complicated out here
[nervous, speaking quickly] got a reading i don't like
[panicked, panting] everything's going to hell. get backup here now
[terrified, voice shaking] took damage. not dead yet though
[low voice, gruff] back in one piece. that's what matters
we made it. could've gone worse
[resigned, flat tone] saw some things out there. don't ask about them yet
[resigned, flat tone] we're back. just leave me alone for now
[low voice, gruff] got banged up but it's nothing fatal
[resigned, flat tone] took a hit. just need doc to patch it
[low voice, gruff] routine. i've done this a thousand times
[low voice, gruff] risky is just another word for interesting
[resigned, flat tone] grim doesn't begin to cover it. but we'll go anyway
[low voice, gruff] they're coming in. stay sharp
clamps hold. we're good
we're loose. heading out
running the preflight. same checks as always
[low voice, gruff] she's away clean. keeping an eye on it
[low voice, gruff] she's down safe. another one in the books
[low voice, gruff] something about the environment doesn't add up
[low voice, gruff] ship's being weird and i don't know why
[low voice, gruff] crew's acting off. makes me uneasy
[urgent, fast pace] we're under fire. this is not what i signed up for
[low voice, gruff] none of this makes any sense to me
